Original Article Title: Quasar Linux (QLNX) – サプライチェーンに潜む静かな足場：ルートキット、PAMバックドア、認証情報収集機能を備えた多機能Linux RATの内部
Summary: Quasar Linux (QLNX) is a sophisticated Linux RAT that employs rootkit capabilities, PAM-based backdoors, and credential harvesting to maintain persistence and steal sensitive data from developer environments. Original Article Title: バイブハッキング：エージェントAIを操る2つの攻撃キャンペーンが中南米の行政機関や金融機関を狙う
Summary: Neo-reGeorg is a tool designed for refactoring reGeorg to improve tunnel connection security and obfuscate traffic. It functions as an HTTP(S) tunneller that enables SOCKS5 proxying through web shells deployed on a target server.

Original Article Title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
Summary: Showboat is a modular Linux post-exploitation framework used by China-linked threat actors, such as Calypso, to target telecommunications providers. The malware functions as a SOCKS5 proxy and remote shell, utilizing encrypted communication and code snippets hosted on Pastebin to maintain persistence and evade detection.

Original Article Title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
Summary: PwC Threat Intelligence has identified a China-based threat actor, Red Lamassu, utilizing a Windows backdoor named JFMBackdoor. The malware is delivered via DLL side-loading and supports various malicious capabilities, including remote shell access, file system operations, and network proxying.

Original Article Title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Linux Rootkits, Router 0-Day, AI Intrusions, Scam Kits and 25 New Stories

Original Article Title: When Identity is the Attack Path
Summary: The 2026 Unit 42 Global Incident Response Report highlights four major trends: AI as a force multiplier for attackers, the dominance of identity-based attacks, expanded software supply chain risks, and evolving nation-state tactics. Original Article Title: Chinese hackers target telcos with new Linux, Windows malware
Summary: The Calypso threat group, also known as Red Lamassu, is conducting a long-term cyber-espionage campaign against telecommunications providers in Asia and the Middle East. The attackers utilize modular Linux and Windows malware, specifically Showboat and JFMBackdoor, to establish persistence, perform network pivoting, and exfiltrate sensitive data.

Original Article Title: Max severity Cisco Secure Workload flaw gives Site Admin privileges
Summary: A maximum-severity vulnerability in Cisco Secure Workload, tracked as CVE-2026-20223, stems from insufficient validation in internal REST APIs. This flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to execute crafted API requests to gain Site Admin privileges and perform unauthorized configuration changes.

Original Article Title: Max severity Cisco Secure Workload flaw gives Site Admin privileges
Summary: A vulnerability in the internal REST API authentication of Cisco Secure Workload allows unauthenticated, remote attackers to gain Site Admin privileges. This flaw enables unauthorized access to site resources, sensitive information, and configuration changes across tenant boundaries.

Original Article Title: Max severity Cisco Secure Workload flaw gives Site Admin privileges
Summary: A vulnerability in Cisco Secure Workload's internal REST APIs allows unauthenticated remote attackers to access site resources with Site Admin privileges. This is caused by insufficient validation and authentication of API requests, potentially leading to unauthorized configuration changes.

Summary: Certify is an offensive security tool designed to enumerate and exploit misconfigurations within Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS). It allows attackers to identify vulnerable certificate templates and potentially escalate privileges within a Windows domain environment.

Summary: Adversaries can abuse Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS) misconfigurations to achieve privilege escalation and persistence within a network. Original Article Title: TP-Link, Photoshop, OpenVPN, Norton VPN vulnerabilities
Summary: The provided text is the official documentation and resource page for Snort, an open-source network intrusion prevention system. It describes the software's functionality, rule sets, and provides links to setup guides and support resources.

Original Article Title: TP-Link, Photoshop, OpenVPN, Norton VPN vulnerabilities
Summary: A stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the tmpServer service of Tp-Link Archer AX53 routers allows remote attackers to achieve arbitrary code execution. By injecting a specially crafted website name into the router's configuration, an attacker can bypass length restrictions and trigger the overflow.

Original Article Title: TP-Link, Photoshop, OpenVPN, Norton VPN vulnerabilities
Summary: A privilege escalation vulnerability exists in the Adobe Photoshop installer (version 2.11.0.30) when installed via the Microsoft Store. An attacker can exploit this by replacing temporary ZIP files with malicious versions, allowing for the execution of arbitrary code with System privileges.

Original Article Title: TP-Link, Photoshop, OpenVPN, Norton VPN vulnerabilities
Summary: A reachable assertion vulnerability in OpenVPN's TLS Crypt v2 Client Key Extraction functionality allows an attacker with a valid client key to trigger a denial of service. By sending a specially crafted packet, an attacker can cause the server to hit an unconditional assertion and terminate all active VPN sessions.

Original Article Title: TP-Link, Photoshop, OpenVPN, Norton VPN vulnerabilities
Summary: The provided text is a vulnerability report listing various security vulnerabilities discovered by Cisco Talos. It includes details on heap-based buffer overflows, command injections, and privilege escalation vulnerabilities across multiple software products.

Original Article Title: TP-Link, Photoshop, OpenVPN, Norton VPN vulnerabilities
Summary: A privilege escalation vulnerability in Norton Secure VPN allows a low-privilege user to perform arbitrary file deletion during the installation process. By creating a junction point in a writable directory, an attacker can trick the installer into deleting system files, potentially leading to elevated privileges.

Original Article Title: From PDB strings to MaaS: Tracking a commodity BadIIS ecosystem used by Chinese-speaking threat

Original Article Title: The time of much patching is coming
Summary: The 'Dirty Frag' vulnerability (CVE-2026-43284 and CVE-2026-43500) is a severe Linux kernel flaw that allows low-privilege users to gain root access by exploiting page cache handling in networking components. Attackers are actively experimenting with the exploit, which chains two vulnerabilities to achieve reliable privilege escalation across major Linux distributions.

Original Article Title: The time of much patching is coming
Summary: The TeamPCP hacker group compromised Checkmarx's GitHub repositories to inject malicious code into the Jenkins Application Security Testing (AST) plugin. This supply-chain attack resulted in the distribution of a rogue plugin version designed to deliver credential-stealing malware.

Original Article Title: The time of much patching is coming
Summary: The Mini Shai-Hulud campaign is a coordinated supply chain attack that compromised over 170 packages across NPM and PyPI ecosystems, including TanStack, Mistral AI, and UiPath. Attackers exploited CI/CD pipeline vulnerabilities and GitHub Actions OIDC tokens to inject malicious code, steal credentials, and exfiltrate data via specific domains and the Session network.

Original Article Title: The time of much patching is coming
Summary: A malicious Hugging Face repository impersonating OpenAI's Privacy Filter was used to deliver a Rust-based information stealer to Windows users. Original Article Title: Ongoing exploitation of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerabilities
Summary: Cisco Talos is tracking multiple threat clusters exploiting vulnerabilities in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN infrastructure, including CVE-2026-20182, CVE-2026-20133, CVE-2026-20128, and CVE-2026-20122. Attackers are deploying various webshells and malicious tools, such as XenShell, Godzilla, Behinder, AdaptixC2, Sliver, and XMRig miners, to gain unauthorized access and execute commands on affected systems.

Original Article Title: 11th May – Threat Intelligence Report
Summary: A vulnerability dubbed ClaudeBleed in the Claude Chrome extension allows malicious extensions to inject prompts and hijack the AI agent. By exploiting lax permissions and improper origin trust, attackers can perform unauthorized actions such as data exfiltration and email manipulation.

The malware harvests credentials from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines, exfiltrating data via GitHub repositories and a dedicated C2 domain.

Original Article Title: 4th May – Threat Intelligence Report
Summary: Threat actors exploited a vulnerability in Robinhood's account creation process to inject malicious HTML into legitimate confirmation emails. This allowed attackers to send convincing phishing messages that directed users to a fraudulent site to steal credentials.

Original Article Title: 4th May – Threat Intelligence Report
Summary: A critical SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-42208) in the LiteLLM Python package allows unauthenticated attackers to access and modify database data. Attackers were observed exploiting this flaw within 36 hours of its public disclosure to enumerate sensitive database schemas.

Original Article Title: 4th May – Threat Intelligence Report
Summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in cPanel (CVE-2026-41940) is being actively exploited in the wild to gain unauthorized administrator-level access. Attackers manipulate session cookies to inject arbitrary properties, allowing them to bypass authentication mechanisms.

Original Article Title: 4th May – Threat Intelligence Report
Summary: A Brazilian DDoS mitigation firm, Huge Networks, was compromised, leading to the exposure of malicious Python scripts and SSH keys used to operate a Mirai-based botnet. The botnet targeted Brazilian ISPs by exploiting vulnerable TP-Link routers to conduct large-scale DNS reflection and amplification attacks.

Original Article Title: Axios NPMパッケージのサプライチェーン侵害によるRATの展開
Summary: Threat actors compromised the Axios npm package by injecting malicious code into versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4, which triggered secondary payload downloads via postinstall scripts. This supply chain attack resulted in the deployment of a Remote Access Trojan (RAT) on developer systems, potentially leading to credential theft and unauthorized remote access.

Threat actors abuse the OAuth device code flow to obtain access and refresh tokens, enabling persistent mailbox access and the establishment of malicious inbox rules to evade detection.

Original Article Title: When trusted apps become trusted backdoors: what the Vercel incident shows about modern account takeover
Summary: Vercel experienced a security incident where an attacker compromised a third-party AI tool's Google Workspace OAuth app to gain unauthorized access to a Vercel employee's account. This allowed the attacker to pivot into Vercel systems and access non-sensitive environment variables.

Original Article Title: Inside agenteV2: How Brazilian Attackers Use Fake Court Summons to Steal Banking Credentials in Real Time
Summary: The agenteV2 banking trojan is a sophisticated, Nuitka-compiled interactive backdoor targeting Brazilian financial institutions and cryptocurrency wallets. It utilizes a multi-stage infection chain, including a VBScript loader and a Pastebin-based dead-drop resolver, to establish a persistent WebSocket connection for real-time screen streaming and remote command execution.

It utilizes a multi-stage infection chain, including a VBScript loader and a Pastebin-based dead-drop resolver, to establish a persistent WebSocket connection for real-time screen streaming and remote command execution.

Original Article Title: WPS Officeで使用される名前付きパイプに対するアクセス制御不備の脆弱性
Summary: WPS Office contains a vulnerability where improper access control on named pipes allows a non-privileged user to execute arbitrary programs with SYSTEM privileges. This issue, identified as CVE-2018-6400, persists in several versions of WPS Office and related products, necessitating an update to the latest version.

Original Article Title: WPS Officeで使用される名前付きパイプに対するアクセス制御不備の脆弱性
Summary: WPS Office contains a vulnerability due to improper access control on named pipes used for inter-process communication. This flaw allows a local user with low privileges to execute arbitrary code with SYSTEM-level permissions.

Original Article Title: WPS Officeで使用される名前付きパイプに対するアクセス制御不備の脆弱性
Summary: WPS Office software contains a vulnerability related to improper access restrictions on named pipes. This flaw could allow unauthorized operations to be executed with PC administrator privileges.

Original Article Title: WPS Officeで使用される名前付きパイプに対するアクセス制御不備の脆弱性
Summary: WPS Office contains a vulnerability where named pipes used for inter-process communication lack proper access control lists (ACLs). This flaw allows a local, non-privileged user to execute arbitrary programs with SYSTEM-level privileges.

The intrusion involves the use of various SaaS platforms for C2, RMM tools for lateral movement, and concludes with the deployment of The Gentlemen ransomware.

Summary: Vidoc Security Lab researchers demonstrated that public AI models, such as GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6, can reproduce vulnerability findings previously attributed to Anthropic's gated 'Mythos' model. The study highlights that advanced AI-assisted vulnerability discovery is already accessible via open-source tooling, shifting the security challenge from model access to validation and operationalization.
